#### 22.10.8 InfinetScreen

**File**: `features/settings/screens/infinet_screen.dart`

Shown when the user is a member of at least one Infinet. If a member of multiple, a top-level list selects which Infinet to manage. Single-Infinet case: go straight to the management view.

**AppBar**: `'${infinet.name}'` (or `'Infinet'` if name not yet loaded).

Tabbed layout (three tabs):

```dart
DefaultTabController(
  length: infinet.canOrchestrate ? 4 : 3,
  child: Scaffold(
    appBar: AppBar(
      title: Text(infinet.name),
      bottom: TabBar(tabs: [
        Tab(text: 'Members'),
        Tab(text: 'Devices'),
        Tab(text: 'Settings'),
        if (infinet.canOrchestrate) Tab(text: 'Tasks'),
      ]),
      actions: [
        if (infinet.isAdmin)
          IconButton(
            icon: Icon(Icons.person_add_outlined),
            tooltip: 'Invite member',
            onPressed: _openInviteSheet,
          ),
      ],
    ),
    body: TabBarView(children: [
      _MembersTab(infinet: infinet),
      _DevicesTab(infinet: infinet),
      _InfinetSettingsTab(infinet: infinet),
      if (infinet.canOrchestrate) _TasksTab(infinet: infinet),
    ]),
  ),
)
```

**_TasksTab** (visible only on devices with `can_orchestrate` capability, §18.6.10):
```dart
Column(children: [
  Padding(
    padding: EdgeInsets.fromLTRB(16, 12, 16, 4),
    child: Row(children: [
      Expanded(child: Text('Tasks', style: textTheme.titleSmall)),
      FilledButton.icon(
        onPressed: () => Navigator.push(context,
          MaterialPageRoute(builder: (_) => TaskCreateScreen(infinet: infinet))),
        icon: Icon(Icons.add, size: 16),
        label: Text('New task'),
        style: FilledButton.styleFrom(
          padding: EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 12),
          minimumSize: Size(0, 36)),
      ),
    ]),
  ),
  if (infinet.activeTasks.isEmpty && infinet.recentTasks.isEmpty)
    Expanded(child: EmptyState(
      icon: Icons.task_alt_outlined,
      title: 'No tasks',
      body: 'Assign a task to a device in your Infinet.',
    ))
  else
    Expanded(child: ListView(
      padding: EdgeInsets.symmetric(vertical: 8),
      children: [
        if (infinet.activeTasks.isNotEmpty) ...[
          Padding(
            padding: EdgeInsets.fromLTRB(16, 4, 16, 4),
            child: Text('Active',
              style: textTheme.labelMedium.copyWith(
                color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant))),
          for (final task in infinet.activeTasks)
            _TaskTile(task: task, onTap: () => _openTaskDetail(task)),
          SizedBox(height: 8),
        ],
        if (infinet.recentTasks.isNotEmpty) ...[
          Padding(
            padding: EdgeInsets.fromLTRB(16, 4, 16, 4),
            child: Text('Recent',
              style: textTheme.labelMedium.copyWith(
                color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant))),
          for (final task in infinet.recentTasks)
            _TaskTile(task: task, onTap: () => _openTaskDetail(task)),
        ],
      ],
    )),
])
```

**_TaskTile:**
```dart
ListTile(
  leading: Container(
    width: 40, height: 40,
    decoration: BoxDecoration(
      color: _taskStatusColor(task.status).withValues(alpha: 0.12),
      borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(10)),
    child: Icon(_taskStatusIcon(task.status), size: 20,
      color: _taskStatusColor(task.status))),
  title: Text(task.name, style: textTheme.titleSmall),
  subtitle: Column(crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start, children: [
    Text('${task.workerName} · ${_taskStatusLabel(task.status)}',
      style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)),
    if (task.progress != null)
      Padding(
        padding: EdgeInsets.only(top: 4),
        child: LinearProgressIndicator(
          value: task.progress,
          minHeight: 3,
          borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(2))),
  ]),
  trailing: task.status == TaskStatus.Running
    ? Text(_formatElapsed(task.startedAt),
        style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(
          color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant, fontFamily: 'monospace'))
    : Text(_formatRelativeTime(task.completedAt ?? task.startedAt),
        style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(
          color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)),
  isThreeLine: task.progress != null,
)
```

Task status colours and icons:
```
Running:           kSecGreen   / Icons.play_circle_outline
Pending:           kSecAmber   / Icons.hourglass_empty_outlined
Completing:        kBrand      / Icons.sync_outlined
Success:           kSecGreen   / Icons.check_circle_outline
Failed:            colorScheme.error / Icons.error_outline
Cancelled:         colorScheme.outline / Icons.cancel_outlined
TimedOut:          kSecAmber   / Icons.timer_off_outlined
Lost:              kSecRed     / Icons.signal_wifi_off_outlined
```

**My Tasks view (worker devices without can_orchestrate):**

Workers see a simplified read-only list of their own active and recent tasks in the Settings section, accessible via a "My tasks" tile that appears in SettingsScreen when the device has active or recently completed tasks.


```dart
ListView.separated(
  padding: EdgeInsets.symmetric(vertical: 8),
  itemCount: infinet.members.length,
  separatorBuilder: (_, __) => Divider(height: 1, indent: 72),
  itemBuilder: (ctx, i) {
    final m = infinet.members[i];
    return ListTile(
      leading: Stack(children: [
        MaskAvatar(name: m.name, size: MaskAvatarSize.medium),
        if (m.isAdmin)
          Positioned(
            right: 0, bottom: 0,
            child: Container(
              width: 14, height: 14,
              decoration: BoxDecoration(
                color: kSecAmber,
                shape: BoxShape.circle,
                border: Border.all(
                  color: colorScheme.surface, width: 1.5)),
              child: Icon(Icons.star_outlined, size: 8, color: Colors.white))),
      ]),
      title: Row(children: [
        Expanded(child: Text(m.name)),
        if (m.isCurrentUser)
          Container(
            padding: EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 6, vertical: 2),
            decoration: BoxDecoration(
              color: kBrand.withValues(alpha: 0.12),
              borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(4)),
            child: Text('You', style: TextStyle(fontSize: 10, color: kBrand))),
      ]),
      subtitle: Text(
        '${m.devices.length} device${m.devices.length == 1 ? '' : 's'}',
        style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(
          color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)),
      trailing: infinet.isAdmin && !m.isCurrentUser
        ? PopupMenuButton<_MemberAction>(
            itemBuilder: (_) => [
              if (!m.isAdmin)
                PopupMenuItem(value: _MemberAction.makeAdmin,
                  child: Text('Make admin')),
              PopupMenuItem(value: _MemberAction.remove,
                child: Text('Remove from Infinet',
                  style: TextStyle(color: colorScheme.error))),
            ],
            onSelected: (action) => _handleMemberAction(action, m),
          )
        : null,
    );
  },
)
```

**Devices tab (_DevicesTab):**
```dart
ListView.separated(
  padding: EdgeInsets.symmetric(vertical: 8),
  itemCount: infinet.allDevices.length,
  separatorBuilder: (_, __) => Divider(height: 1, indent: 72),
  itemBuilder: (ctx, i) {
    final d = infinet.allDevices[i];
    return ListTile(
      leading: Container(
        width: 40, height: 40,
        decoration: BoxDecoration(
          color: colorScheme.surfaceContainerHighest,
          borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(10)),
        child: Icon(Icons.computer_outlined, size: 22,
          color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)),
      title: Text(d.shortname),
      subtitle: Column(
        crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start,
        children: [
          Text('${d.ownerName} · ${d.ipv4 ?? d.ipv6 ?? 'No address'}',
            style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(
              color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)),
          if (d.services.isNotEmpty)
            Text(d.services.map((s) => s.name).join(', '),
              style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(
                color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant, fontSize: 11)),
        ],
      ),
      trailing: Text(
        d.lastSeen != null ? _formatRelativeTime(d.lastSeen!) : 'Unknown',
        style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(
          color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)),
    );
  },
)
```

**Settings tab (_InfinetSettingsTab) — admin view:**
```dart
ListView(
  padding: EdgeInsets.all(16),
  children: [

    // ACL rules
    _SectionHeader('Access control'),
    for (final rule in infinet.acl)
      _AclRuleTile(rule: rule, infinet: infinet,
        onDelete: infinet.isAdmin ? () => infinet.deleteAcl(rule.ruleId) : null),
    if (infinet.isAdmin)
      ListTile(
        leading: Icon(Icons.add_circle_outline),
        title: Text('Add ACL rule'),
        onTap: _openAclSheet,
      ),

    Divider(height: 1),

    // DNS records
    _SectionHeader('Custom DNS records'),
    for (final rec in infinet.dnsRecords)
      _DnsRecordTile(record: rec, infinet: infinet,
        onDelete: infinet.isAdmin ? () => infinet.deleteDns(rec.recordId) : null),
    if (infinet.isAdmin)
      ListTile(
        leading: Icon(Icons.add_circle_outline),
        title: Text('Add DNS record'),
        onTap: _openDnsSheet,
      ),

    Divider(height: 1),

    // Join policy (admin only)
    if (infinet.isAdmin) ...[
      _SectionHeader('Join policy'),
      RadioListTile<JoinPolicy>(
        title: Text('Admin approval required'),
        subtitle: Text('Any admin can approve join requests.'),
        value: JoinPolicy.adminApproval,
        groupValue: infinet.joinPolicy,
        onChanged: (v) => infinet.setJoinPolicy(v!),
      ),
      RadioListTile<JoinPolicy>(
        title: Text('Quorum approval'),
        subtitle: Text('Most admins must approve.'),
        value: JoinPolicy.quorum,
        groupValue: infinet.joinPolicy,
        onChanged: (v) => infinet.setJoinPolicy(v!),
      ),
      RadioListTile<JoinPolicy>(
        title: Text('Auto-approve trusted peers'),
        subtitle: Text('Level 6+ contacts join automatically.'),
        value: JoinPolicy.autoApproveTrusted,
        groupValue: infinet.joinPolicy,
        onChanged: (v) => infinet.setJoinPolicy(v!),
      ),
      RadioListTile<JoinPolicy>(
        title: Text('Invite only'),
        subtitle: Text('Requires a one-time invite token.'),
        value: JoinPolicy.inviteOnly,
        groupValue: infinet.joinPolicy,
        onChanged: (v) => infinet.setJoinPolicy(v!),
      ),

      Divider(height: 1),

      // Leave / dissolve
      Padding(
        padding: EdgeInsets.symmetric(vertical: 8),
        child: OutlinedButton.icon(
          onPressed: _confirmLeave,
          icon: Icon(Icons.exit_to_app_outlined),
          label: Text('Leave Infinet'),
          style: OutlinedButton.styleFrom(
            foregroundColor: colorScheme.error,
            minimumSize: Size(double.infinity, 44)),
        ),
      ),
    ],

    SizedBox(height: 24),
  ],
)
```

**Add ACL rule sheet** (`_openAclSheet`):
```dart
Column(mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.min, children: [
  BottomSheetHandle(),
  Padding(
    padding: EdgeInsets.fromLTRB(16, 8, 16, 32),
    child: Column(crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start, children: [
      Text('Add ACL rule', style: textTheme.titleMedium),
      SizedBox(height: 16),

      // Subject
      Text('Who', style: textTheme.labelMedium),
      SizedBox(height: 8),
      DropdownButton<AclSubjectType>(
        isExpanded: true,
        value: _subjectType,
        items: [
          DropdownMenuItem(value: AclSubjectType.everyone,
            child: Text('Everyone in Infinet')),
          DropdownMenuItem(value: AclSubjectType.member,
            child: Text('Specific member')),
          DropdownMenuItem(value: AclSubjectType.role,
            child: Text('By role')),
        ],
        onChanged: (v) => setState(() => _subjectType = v!),
      ),
      if (_subjectType == AclSubjectType.member)
        _MemberPicker(members: infinet.members, onSelected: (m) => _subject = m),

      SizedBox(height: 16),

      // Target
      Text('What', style: textTheme.labelMedium),
      SizedBox(height: 8),
      DropdownButton<AclTargetType>(
        isExpanded: true,
        value: _targetType,
        items: [
          DropdownMenuItem(value: AclTargetType.everything,
            child: Text('All devices and services')),
          DropdownMenuItem(value: AclTargetType.device,
            child: Text('Specific device')),
          DropdownMenuItem(value: AclTargetType.service,
            child: Text('Specific service')),
          DropdownMenuItem(value: AclTargetType.subnet,
            child: Text('IP subnet')),
        ],
        onChanged: (v) => setState(() => _targetType = v!),
      ),

      SizedBox(height: 16),

      // Permission
      Text('Permission', style: textTheme.labelMedium),
      SizedBox(height: 8),
      SegmentedButton<AclPermission>(
        segments: [
          ButtonSegment(value: AclPermission.allow,
            label: Text('Allow'), icon: Icon(Icons.check_outlined, size: 14)),
          ButtonSegment(value: AclPermission.deny,
            label: Text('Deny'), icon: Icon(Icons.block_outlined, size: 14)),
        ],
        selected: {_permission},
        onSelectionChanged: (s) => setState(() => _permission = s.first),
      ),

      SizedBox(height: 20),
      FilledButton(
        onPressed: _canSave ? _saveRule : null,
        style: FilledButton.styleFrom(minimumSize: Size(double.infinity, 44)),
        child: Text('Add rule'),
      ),
    ]),
  ),
])
```

**Add DNS record sheet** (`_openDnsSheet`):
```dart
Column(mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.min, children: [
  BottomSheetHandle(),
  Padding(
    padding: EdgeInsets.fromLTRB(16, 8, 16, 32),
    child: Column(children: [
      Text('Add DNS record', style: textTheme.titleMedium),
      SizedBox(height: 16),
      TextField(
        controller: _nameController,
        decoration: InputDecoration(
          labelText: 'Name',
          hintText: 'e.g. git',
          suffixText: '.${infinet.dnsZone}',
        ),
      ),
      SizedBox(height: 12),
      DropdownButton<DnsRecordType>(
        isExpanded: true,
        value: _recordType,
        items: [
          DropdownMenuItem(value: DnsRecordType.a, child: Text('A')),
          DropdownMenuItem(value: DnsRecordType.aaaa, child: Text('AAAA')),
          DropdownMenuItem(value: DnsRecordType.cname, child: Text('CNAME')),
          DropdownMenuItem(value: DnsRecordType.txt, child: Text('TXT')),
        ],
        onChanged: (v) => setState(() => _recordType = v!),
      ),
      SizedBox(height: 12),
      TextField(
        controller: _valueController,
        decoration: InputDecoration(labelText: 'Value'),
      ),
      SizedBox(height: 20),
      FilledButton(
        onPressed: _canSave ? _saveDnsRecord : null,
        style: FilledButton.styleFrom(minimumSize: Size(double.infinity, 44)),
        child: Text('Add record'),
      ),
    ]),
  ),
])
```

---

#### 22.10.8a TaskDetailScreen

**File:** `features/infinet/screens/task_detail_screen.dart`

Reached by tapping a `_TaskTile` in the Infinet Tasks tab. Shows live status, audit trail, and task controls.

**AppBar:** `task.name`, trailing `IconButton(Icons.cancel_outlined)` (Cancel task — only shown when `task.status == Running || Pending`).

```dart
ListView(padding: EdgeInsets.all(16), children: [

  // Status card
  Card(child: Padding(padding: EdgeInsets.all(16), child: Column(
    crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start, children: [
      Row(children: [
        Container(
          width: 36, height: 36,
          decoration: BoxDecoration(
            color: _taskStatusColor(task.status).withValues(alpha: 0.12),
            borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(8)),
          child: Icon(_taskStatusIcon(task.status), size: 20,
            color: _taskStatusColor(task.status))),
        SizedBox(width: 12),
        Expanded(child: Column(crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start,
          children: [
            Text(_taskStatusLabel(task.status),
              style: textTheme.titleSmall.copyWith(
                color: _taskStatusColor(task.status))),
            Text('Worker: ${task.workerName}',
              style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(
                color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)),
          ])),
      ]),

      if (task.progress != null) ...[
        SizedBox(height: 12),
        LinearProgressIndicator(value: task.progress, minHeight: 4,
          borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(2)),
        SizedBox(height: 4),
        Text('${(task.progress! * 100).toStringAsFixed(0)}%',
          style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(
            color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)),
      ],

      SizedBox(height: 12),
      // Timing
      _InfoRow(label: 'Started', value: _formatAbsTime(task.startedAt)),
      if (task.completedAt != null)
        _InfoRow(label: 'Completed', value: _formatAbsTime(task.completedAt!)),
      if (task.deadline != null)
        _InfoRow(label: 'Deadline', value: _formatAbsTime(task.deadline!)),
    ],
  ))),

  SizedBox(height: 12),

  // Resource usage (live for Running, snapshot for completed)
  if (task.resourceUsage != null)
    Card(child: Padding(padding: EdgeInsets.all(16), child: Column(
      crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start, children: [
        Text('Resource usage', style: textTheme.titleSmall),
        SizedBox(height: 8),
        _ResourceBar(label: 'CPU', value: task.resourceUsage!.cpuPercent,
          max: 100, unit: '%'),
        _ResourceBar(label: 'Memory',
          value: task.resourceUsage!.memoryMb.toDouble(),
          max: task.budget.maxMemoryMb?.toDouble() ?? 8192,
          unit: 'MB'),
        _InfoRow(label: 'Network sent',
          value: '${task.resourceUsage!.netSentMb} MB'),
        _InfoRow(label: 'Network received',
          value: '${task.resourceUsage!.netRecvMb} MB'),
      ],
    ))),

  SizedBox(height: 12),

  // Access grants
  SectionHeader('Access grants'),
  for (final grant in task.accessGrants)
    ListTile(
      dense: true,
      leading: Icon(
        grant.scope == TaskAccessScope.readOnly
          ? Icons.download_outlined
          : grant.scope == TaskAccessScope.writeOnly
            ? Icons.upload_outlined
            : Icons.sync_outlined,
        size: 18, color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant),
      title: Text(grant.target.description,
        style: textTheme.bodySmall),
      trailing: Container(
        padding: EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 6, vertical: 2),
        decoration: BoxDecoration(
          color: colorScheme.surfaceContainerHighest,
          borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(4)),
        child: Text(grant.scope.label,
          style: textTheme.labelSmall))),

  SizedBox(height: 12),

  // Log tail (live for Running, full for completed)
  SectionHeader('Activity log'),
  Container(
    height: 180,
    decoration: BoxDecoration(
      color: colorScheme.surfaceContainerHighest,
      borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(8)),
    child: ListView.builder(
      controller: _logScrollController,
      padding: EdgeInsets.all(10),
      itemCount: task.logLines.length,
      itemBuilder: (ctx, i) => Text(task.logLines[i],
        style: TextStyle(
          fontFamily: 'monospace', fontSize: 11,
          color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)))),

  SizedBox(height: 24),

  // Cancel button (Running/Pending only)
  if (task.status == TaskStatus.running ||
      task.status == TaskStatus.pending)
    OutlinedButton.icon(
      onPressed: _confirmCancel,
      icon: Icon(Icons.cancel_outlined),
      label: Text('Cancel task'),
      style: OutlinedButton.styleFrom(
        foregroundColor: colorScheme.error,
        minimumSize: Size(double.infinity, 48))),

  SizedBox(height: 24),
])
```

Cancel confirmation:
```dart
AlertDialog(
  title: Text('Cancel task?'),
  content: Text(
    'The worker will be asked to stop within 5 seconds. '
    'Partial results may not be available.'),
  actions: [
    TextButton(onPressed: () => Navigator.pop(context), child: Text('Keep running')),
    FilledButton(
      onPressed: () { Navigator.pop(context); infinet.cancelTask(task.id); },
      style: FilledButton.styleFrom(backgroundColor: colorScheme.error),
      child: Text('Cancel task')),
  ],
)
```

---

#### 22.10.8b TaskCreateScreen

**File:** `features/infinet/screens/task_create_screen.dart`

**AppBar:** `'New task'`, leading `CloseButton`.

```dart
ListView(padding: EdgeInsets.all(16), children: [

  // Task name and description
  TextField(
    controller: _nameController,
    decoration: InputDecoration(labelText: 'Task name'),
  ),
  SizedBox(height: 8),
  TextField(
    controller: _descController,
    maxLines: 2,
    decoration: InputDecoration(
      labelText: 'Description (optional)',
      alignLabelWithHint: true),
  ),

  SizedBox(height: 16),
  SectionHeader('Task type'),

  // Task type selector — segmented button
  SegmentedButton<TaskTypeVariant>(
    segments: [
      ButtonSegment(value: TaskTypeVariant.shell,
        icon: Icon(Icons.terminal_outlined), label: Text('Shell')),
      ButtonSegment(value: TaskTypeVariant.fileTransform,
        icon: Icon(Icons.transform_outlined), label: Text('Transform')),
      ButtonSegment(value: TaskTypeVariant.httpRequest,
        icon: Icon(Icons.http_outlined), label: Text('HTTP')),
    ],
    selected: {_taskType},
    onSelectionChanged: (s) => setState(() => _taskType = s.first),
  ),

  SizedBox(height: 12),

  // Type-specific fields
  if (_taskType == TaskTypeVariant.shell) ...[
    TextField(
      controller: _commandController,
      style: TextStyle(fontFamily: 'monospace', fontSize: 13),
      decoration: InputDecoration(
        labelText: 'Command',
        hintText: 'e.g. ffmpeg -i input.mp4 output.webm'),
    ),
  ],
  if (_taskType == TaskTypeVariant.fileTransform) ...[
    TextField(controller: _inputPathController,
      decoration: InputDecoration(labelText: 'Input path')),
    SizedBox(height: 8),
    TextField(controller: _outputPathController,
      decoration: InputDecoration(labelText: 'Output path')),
    SizedBox(height: 8),
    TextField(controller: _transformController,
      decoration: InputDecoration(
        labelText: 'Transform',
        hintText: 'e.g. transcode:h264, resize:1920x1080')),
  ],
  if (_taskType == TaskTypeVariant.httpRequest) ...[
    DropdownButtonFormField<String>(
      value: _httpMethod,
      items: ['GET','POST','PUT','PATCH','DELETE']
        .map((m) => DropdownMenuItem(value: m, child: Text(m))).toList(),
      onChanged: (v) => setState(() => _httpMethod = v!),
      decoration: InputDecoration(labelText: 'Method'),
    ),
    SizedBox(height: 8),
    TextField(controller: _urlController,
      decoration: InputDecoration(labelText: 'URL')),
  ],

  SizedBox(height: 16),
  SectionHeader('Worker device'),

  // Device picker — shows purpose devices with capability match indicator
  if (infinet.purposeDevices.isEmpty)
    Padding(
      padding: EdgeInsets.symmetric(vertical: 8),
      child: Text('No purpose devices declared. Add purposes in Infinet settings.',
        style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)))
  else
    for (final device in infinet.purposeDevices)
      RadioListTile<String>(
        value: device.id,
        groupValue: _selectedWorker,
        onChanged: (v) => setState(() => _selectedWorker = v),
        title: Text(device.name),
        subtitle: Text(device.purposes.map((p) => p.tag).join(', '),
          style: textTheme.bodySmall),
        secondary: Container(
          width: 10, height: 10,
          decoration: BoxDecoration(
            color: device.isOnline ? kSecGreen : colorScheme.outline,
            shape: BoxShape.circle)),
      ),

  SizedBox(height: 16),
  SectionHeader('Resource budget'),
  Text('Optional. Leave blank for no limit.',
    style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)),
  SizedBox(height: 8),

  Row(children: [
    Expanded(child: TextField(
      controller: _maxCpuController,
      keyboardType: TextInputType.number,
      decoration: InputDecoration(labelText: 'Max CPU %', suffixText: '%'),
    )),
    SizedBox(width: 12),
    Expanded(child: TextField(
      controller: _maxMemController,
      keyboardType: TextInputType.number,
      decoration: InputDecoration(labelText: 'Max RAM', suffixText: 'MB'),
    )),
  ]),

  SizedBox(height: 16),
  SectionHeader('Access grants'),
  Padding(
    padding: EdgeInsets.only(bottom: 8),
    child: Text(
      'What this task is allowed to access on the Infinet. '
      'Prefer the minimum necessary.',
      style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant))),

  for (final grant in _accessGrants)
    ListTile(
      dense: true,
      leading: Icon(Icons.lock_outline, size: 16),
      title: Text(grant.target.description, style: textTheme.bodySmall),
      trailing: IconButton(
        icon: Icon(Icons.close, size: 16),
        onPressed: () => setState(() => _accessGrants.remove(grant)))),

  OutlinedButton.icon(
    onPressed: _openAclGrantSheet,
    icon: Icon(Icons.add, size: 16),
    label: Text('Add access grant')),

  SizedBox(height: 16),
  SectionHeader('Deadline'),
  ListTile(
    contentPadding: EdgeInsets.zero,
    title: Text(_deadline == null ? 'No deadline' : _formatDeadline(_deadline!)),
    trailing: Row(mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.min, children: [
      if (_deadline != null)
        IconButton(icon: Icon(Icons.close, size: 16),
          onPressed: () => setState(() => _deadline = null)),
      TextButton(onPressed: _pickDeadline, child: Text('Set')),
    ])),

  SizedBox(height: 24),

  FilledButton(
    onPressed: _canCreate ? _createTask : null,
    style: FilledButton.styleFrom(minimumSize: Size(double.infinity, 52)),
    child: Text('Create task')),

  SizedBox(height: 24),
])
```

`_canCreate` = `_nameController.text.isNotEmpty && _selectedWorker != null && _commandController.text.isNotEmpty` (adjusted per task type).

`_createTask()` calls `infinet.createTask(spec)`, pops on success with `SnackBar`: *"Task created and sent to [worker]."*

---

#### 22.10.9 NodeManagementScreen

**File**: `features/settings/screens/node_management_screen.dart`

Only accessible when the node is in Dual or Server mode. Provides in-app visibility into the management interface defined in §17.12.

**AppBar**: `'Node Management'`.

```dart
Scaffold(
  appBar: AppBar(title: Text('Node Management')),
  body: ListView(
    padding: EdgeInsets.all(16),
    children: [

      // Node mode + identity
      Card(child: Padding(
        padding: EdgeInsets.all(16),
        child: Column(crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start, children: [
          Row(children: [
            Icon(Icons.dns_outlined, size: 20, color: kBrand),
            SizedBox(width: 8),
            Text(node.modeName, style: textTheme.titleSmall),
            Spacer(),
            Container(
              padding: EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 8, vertical: 3),
              decoration: BoxDecoration(
                color: node.isOnline ? kSecGreen.withValues(alpha: 0.12) : colorScheme.errorContainer,
                borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(4)),
              child: Text(
                node.isOnline ? 'Online' : 'Degraded',
                style: TextStyle(
                  fontSize: 11,
                  color: node.isOnline ? kSecGreen : colorScheme.error,
                  fontWeight: FontWeight.w600)),
            ),
          ]),
          SizedBox(height: 8),
          SelectableText(node.meshAddress,
            style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(fontFamily: 'monospace')),
        ]),
      )),

      SizedBox(height: 12),

      // Health summary
      _ManagementCard(
        title: 'Health',
        children: [
          _MgmtRow(Icons.memory_outlined, 'Memory', node.memoryUsage),
          _MgmtRow(Icons.speed_outlined, 'CPU', node.cpuUsage),
          _MgmtRow(Icons.swap_vert_outlined, 'Bandwidth', node.bandwidthSummary),
          _MgmtRow(Icons.hub_outlined, 'Active tunnels', '${node.tunnelCount}'),
          _MgmtRow(Icons.inbox_outlined, 'S&F queue depth', '${node.sfQueueDepth}'),
        ],
      ),

      SizedBox(height: 12),

      // Plugins
      _ManagementCard(
        title: 'Plugins',
        trailing: TextButton(
          onPressed: () => Navigator.push(context, route(PluginsScreen())),
          child: Text('Manage')),
        children: [
          if (node.installedPlugins.isEmpty)
            Padding(
              padding: EdgeInsets.symmetric(vertical: 8),
              child: Text('No plugins installed.',
                style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(
                  color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)))
          else
            for (final p in node.installedPlugins)
              _PluginRow(plugin: p),
        ],
      ),

      SizedBox(height: 12),

      // Storage
      _ManagementCard(
        title: 'Storage',
        trailing: TextButton(
          onPressed: () => Navigator.push(context, route(StorageSettingsScreen())),
          child: Text('Configure')),
        children: [
          _MgmtRow(Icons.folder_outlined, 'Vault size', node.vaultSizeStr),
          _MgmtRow(Icons.schedule_outlined, 'Message retention',
            node.messageRetentionStr),
          _MgmtRow(Icons.map_outlined, 'Network map entries',
            '${node.networkMapEntries}'),
        ],
      ),

      SizedBox(height: 12),

      // Access paths
      _ManagementCard(
        title: 'Management access',
        children: [
          ListTile(
            contentPadding: EdgeInsets.zero,
            leading: Icon(Icons.lock_outlined, size: 20),
            title: Text('Local HTTPS'),
            subtitle: Text(node.localManagementUrl ?? 'Not configured'),
            trailing: TextButton(
              onPressed: node.localManagementUrl != null
                ? () => _openUrl(node.localManagementUrl!)
                : null,
              child: Text('Open')),
          ),
          ListTile(
            contentPadding: EdgeInsets.zero,
            leading: Icon(Icons.hub_outlined, size: 20),
            title: Text('Mesh control plane'),
            subtitle: Text('${node.meshAdmins.length} admin identities configured'),
            trailing: TextButton(
              onPressed: () => Navigator.push(context, route(MeshAdminScreen())),
              child: Text('Configure')),
          ),
        ],
      ),

      SizedBox(height: 24),
    ],
  ),
)
```

**_ManagementCard:**
```dart
Card(child: Padding(
  padding: EdgeInsets.all(16),
  child: Column(crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start, children: [
    Row(children: [
      Expanded(child: Text(title, style: textTheme.titleSmall)),
      if (trailing != null) trailing!,
    ]),
    SizedBox(height: 8),
    ...children,
  ]),
))
```

**_MgmtRow:**
```dart
Padding(
  padding: EdgeInsets.symmetric(vertical: 3),
  child: Row(children: [
    Icon(icon, size: 16, color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant),
    SizedBox(width: 8),
    Expanded(child: Text(label, style: textTheme.bodySmall)),
    Text(value, style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(
      fontWeight: FontWeight.w600)),
  ]),
)
```

#### 22.10.10 PinScreen

**File**: `features/settings/screens/pin_screen.dart`

Handles PIN setup, change, and removal. Also used as the PIN entry gate when the app re-locks.

**Modes** (determined by `PinScreenMode` enum passed as route argument):

`setup` — First-time PIN creation. Shows two-step entry (enter → confirm).
`change` — Change existing PIN. Shows three-step (verify old → enter new → confirm new).
`remove` — Remove PIN. Shows one-step verification then confirmation.
`unlock` — Re-authentication gate. Shows entry field only. No AppBar close button.

**AppBar** (shown in `setup`/`change`/`remove` modes): `'App PIN'`, leading `CloseButton` (disabled in `unlock` mode).

**PIN entry widget (`_PinEntry`):**

```dart
Column(children: [

  Text(stepLabel, style: textTheme.titleMedium, textAlign: TextAlign.center),
  SizedBox(height: 6),
  Text(stepSubtitle,
    style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant),
    textAlign: TextAlign.center),

  SizedBox(height: 28),

  // PIN dots — 4 filled/unfilled circles showing entry progress
  Row(mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center, children: [
    for (int i = 0; i < pin.maxLength; i++)
      AnimatedContainer(
        duration: Duration(milliseconds: 150),
        margin: EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 6),
        width: 14, height: 14,
        decoration: BoxDecoration(
          color: i < pin.entered
            ? colorScheme.primary
            : colorScheme.outline.withValues(alpha: 0.3),
          shape: BoxShape.circle),
      ),
  ]),

  SizedBox(height: 28),

  // Hidden text field (auto-focus, numeric keyboard)
  Opacity(
    opacity: 0,
    child: TextField(
      controller: _pinController,
      autofocus: true,
      keyboardType: TextInputType.numberWithOptions(signed: false, decimal: false),
      obscureText: true,
      maxLength: 16,
    ),
  ),
])
```

The visible PIN dots are driven by `_pinController`. The hidden field captures keyboard input on all platforms. On mobile, tapping anywhere on the PIN area re-focuses the hidden field.

**PIN length:** 4–16 digits. The dots expand to show actual entry count. Below 4, the confirm button is disabled.

**step labels and subtitles by mode:**
```
setup / step 1:  "Create a PIN"           / "4 to 16 digits"
setup / step 2:  "Confirm your PIN"       / "Enter the same PIN again"
setup / success: ← navigate back, show SnackBar "PIN set"

change / step 1: "Enter your current PIN" / ""
change / step 2: "Enter new PIN"          / "4 to 16 digits"
change / step 3: "Confirm new PIN"        / "Enter the same PIN again"
change / success: ← navigate back, show SnackBar "PIN changed"

remove / step 1: "Enter your PIN to remove it" / ""
remove / success: ← navigate back, show SnackBar "PIN removed"

unlock / step 1: "Enter your PIN"         / ""
unlock / success: → pop and continue to app
```

**Wrong PIN:** Shake animation on the dots row (150ms left-right oscillation, 3 cycles), `kSecRed` fill colour for 400ms, then reset. After 5 failed attempts, a 30-second lockout timer is displayed.

**Duress PIN (if configured, §3.10):** The duress PIN is entered identically to the normal PIN. The system backend detects it via the `bridge.unlockIdentity()` return value — the UI never knows it was a duress unlock. This is by design: the UI must look completely normal.

**Forgot PIN:** Link shown in `unlock` mode below the dots after the first wrong attempt. Shows a bottom sheet with two recovery paths:

```dart
BottomSheet(
  child: Column(mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.min, children: [
    Padding(
      padding: EdgeInsets.fromLTRB(20, 16, 20, 4),
      child: Text('Forgot your PIN?', style: textTheme.titleMedium)),
    Padding(
      padding: EdgeInsets.fromLTRB(20, 0, 20, 16),
      child: Text(
        'Your PIN protects your identity and messages. Without it, '
        'there is no way to recover your existing data on this device.',
        style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant))),
    ListTile(
      leading: Icon(Icons.restore_outlined, color: kBrand),
      title: Text('Erase and restore from backup'),
      subtitle: Text('If you have a backup, you can erase this device '
        'and restore your identity and history from it.'),
      trailing: Icon(Icons.chevron_right),
      onTap: () => _eraseAndRestore(),
    ),
    ListTile(
      leading: Icon(Icons.delete_forever_outlined, color: colorScheme.error),
      title: Text('Erase and start fresh'),
      subtitle: Text('Permanently delete everything on this device. '
        'Create a new identity from scratch.'),
      trailing: Icon(Icons.chevron_right),
      onTap: () => _eraseAndStartFresh(),
    ),
    SizedBox(height: 8),
  ]),
)
```

`_eraseAndRestore()` triggers the emergency erase (§3.9.1) then immediately opens the onboarding Import Backup screen (§22.42.2). `_eraseAndStartFresh()` triggers the emergency erase then opens the normal Welcome screen. Both require a confirmation dialog before proceeding.

**Duress PIN test flow** (`_openDuressTest` from EmergencyEraseScreen):

Opens a PinScreen in `testDuress` mode. The user enters their duress PIN. The backend verifies it matches the configured duress PIN without triggering any erase. On success: `SnackBar` with "Duress PIN verified — it works." and haptic `notificationSuccess`. On failure: standard wrong-PIN shake animation and `SnackBar` "That's not your duress PIN."

---

#### 22.10.11 EmergencyEraseScreen

**File**: `features/settings/screens/emergency_erase_screen.dart`

**AppBar**: `'Emergency Erase'`.

The emergency erase has two parts: configuration (done in advance, accessible from Settings) and activation (done in an emergency). This screen handles both.

**Configuration view** (reached from Settings when erase is not yet configured or when reconfiguring):

```dart
ListView(
  padding: EdgeInsets.all(16),
  children: [

    // Explanation card
    Card(child: Padding(
      padding: EdgeInsets.all(16),
      child: Column(crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start, children: [
        Row(children: [
          Icon(Icons.emergency_outlined, size: 20, color: colorScheme.error),
          SizedBox(width: 8),
          Text('What this does', style: textTheme.titleSmall),
        ]),
        SizedBox(height: 8),
        Text(
          'Emergency erase immediately deletes your identity, keys, and all '
          'message history. It also broadcasts a "self-disavowed" signal to '
          'your contacts so they know not to trust further messages from this '
          'device.\n\n'
          'Configure a trigger now — you want this ready before you need it.',
          style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(
            color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)),
      ]),
    )),

    SizedBox(height: 16),

    // Duress PIN configuration
    SectionHeader('Duress PIN'),
    Padding(
      padding: EdgeInsets.fromLTRB(16, 0, 16, 8),
      child: Text(
        'A second PIN that looks like a normal unlock but silently erases '
        'everything. An observer cannot tell the difference.',
        style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)),
    ),
    ListTile(
      leading: Icon(Icons.pin_outlined),
      title: Text(settings.duressPinConfigured
        ? 'Duress PIN configured'
        : 'Set duress PIN'),
      subtitle: Text(settings.duressPinConfigured
        ? 'Tap to change'
        : 'Tap to configure'),
      trailing: Icon(Icons.chevron_right),
      onTap: () => Navigator.push(context,
        MaterialPageRoute(builder: (_) =>
          PinScreen(mode: PinScreenMode.setupDuress))),
    ),
    if (settings.duressPinConfigured)
      ListTile(
        leading: Icon(Icons.science_outlined),
        title: Text('Test duress PIN'),
        subtitle: Text('Verify your duress PIN works without erasing anything.'),
        trailing: Icon(Icons.chevron_right),
        onTap: () => _openDuressTest(),
      ),

    Divider(height: 1),

    // Wrong PIN threshold
    SectionHeader('Auto-wipe on wrong PIN'),
    Padding(
      padding: EdgeInsets.fromLTRB(16, 0, 16, 8),
      child: Text(
        'Automatically erase after too many wrong PIN attempts. '
        'Disabled by default.',
        style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)),
    ),
    SwitchListTile(
      title: Text('Enable wrong-PIN wipe'),
      value: settings.wrongPinWipeEnabled,
      onChanged: (v) => settings.setWrongPinWipe(v),
    ),
    if (settings.wrongPinWipeEnabled)
      ListTile(
        title: Text('Wipe after'),
        trailing: DropdownButton<int>(
          value: settings.wrongPinWipeThreshold,
          items: [3, 5, 10].map((n) => DropdownMenuItem(
            value: n,
            child: Text('$n attempts'))).toList(),
          onChanged: (v) => settings.setWrongPinThreshold(v!),
        ),
      ),

    Divider(height: 1),

    // Remote wipe
    SectionHeader('Remote trigger'),
    Padding(
      padding: EdgeInsets.fromLTRB(16, 0, 16, 8),
      child: Text(
        'A trusted contact with InnerCircle (Level 8) status can trigger '
        'an erase remotely if you\'re unreachable.',
        style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)),
    ),
    SwitchListTile(
      title: Text('Allow remote erase'),
      subtitle: Text(settings.remoteWipeEnabled
        ? '${settings.remoteWipeApproversCount} Inner Circle contact(s) authorised'
        : 'Off'),
      value: settings.remoteWipeEnabled,
      onChanged: (v) => settings.setRemoteWipe(v),
    ),

    SizedBox(height: 32),

    // Manual activation
    Padding(
      padding: EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 0),
      child: OutlinedButton.icon(
        onPressed: _confirmManualErase,
        icon: Icon(Icons.delete_forever_outlined),
        label: Text('Erase now'),
        style: OutlinedButton.styleFrom(
          foregroundColor: colorScheme.error,
          minimumSize: Size(double.infinity, 52),
        ),
      ),
    ),

    SizedBox(height: 16),
  ],
)
```

**Manual erase confirmation dialog:**
```dart
AlertDialog(
  // barrierDismissible: false
  title: Row(children: [
    Icon(Icons.warning_rounded, color: colorScheme.error),
    SizedBox(width: 8),
    Text('Erase everything?'),
  ]),
  content: Column(mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.min, children: [
    Text(
      'This will immediately and permanently delete:\n'
      '• Your identity and all keys\n'
      '• All messages and conversations\n'
      '• All contacts and trust relationships\n\n'
      'Your contacts will be notified that this device has been erased. '
      'This cannot be undone.',
      style: textTheme.bodySmall),
    SizedBox(height: 16),
    // PIN re-authentication required
    TextField(
      controller: _pinController,
      keyboardType: TextInputType.numberWithOptions(),
      obscureText: true,
      decoration: InputDecoration(
        labelText: 'Enter your PIN to confirm',
        errorText: _pinError,
      ),
    ),
  ]),
  actions: [
    TextButton(onPressed: () => Navigator.pop(context), child: Text('Cancel')),
    FilledButton(
      onPressed: _pinController.text.length >= 4 ? _executeErase : null,
      style: FilledButton.styleFrom(backgroundColor: colorScheme.error),
      child: Text('Erase everything')),
  ],
)
```

After confirmed erase: full-screen loading with `CircularProgressIndicator` and text *"Erasing…"* — no cancel possible. On completion, app restarts to onboarding.

---

#### 22.10.12 CallScreen

**File**: `features/calling/screens/call_screen.dart`

Voice and video calls. Reached via the call action in ThreadScreen or ContactDetailScreen. Full-screen experience.

**Layout:** Full-screen scaffold. No AppBar. `SafeArea`.

```dart
Scaffold(
  backgroundColor: const Color(0xFF0F1117),  // always dark, regardless of theme
  body: SafeArea(
    child: Stack(children: [

      // Video background (full screen) — only in video calls
      if (isVideoCall && remoteVideoActive)
        Positioned.fill(child: _RemoteVideoView()),
      else
        // Audio call background: gradient from kBrand dark to scaffold
        Positioned.fill(child: Container(
          decoration: BoxDecoration(gradient: LinearGradient(
            begin: Alignment.topCenter,
            end: Alignment.bottomCenter,
            colors: [kBrandDark.withValues(alpha: 0.4), Color(0xFF0F1117)],
          )),
        )),

      // Local video preview (PiP) — video call only
      if (isVideoCall && localVideoActive)
        Positioned(
          top: 16, right: 16,
          child: GestureDetector(
            onPanUpdate: _movePip,
            child: ClipRRect(
              borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(12),
              child: SizedBox(width: 100, height: 140,
                child: _LocalVideoView())),
          ),
        ),

      // Main content column
      Column(children: [
        SizedBox(height: 24),

        // Contact info
        _ContactAvatar(peer: contact, size: 80),
        SizedBox(height: 12),
        Text(contact.resolvedDisplayName,
          style: textTheme.titleLarge.copyWith(color: Colors.white)),
        SizedBox(height: 6),
        Text(_callStatusLabel(callState),
          style: textTheme.bodyMedium.copyWith(
            color: Colors.white.withValues(alpha: 0.7))),
        // callStatusLabel: 'Calling…' | 'Connecting…' | '00:42' (timer) | 'Call ended'

        // Connection mode indicator
        if (call.connectionMode == ConnectionMode.loSec)
          Container(
            margin: EdgeInsets.only(top: 6),
            padding: EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 10, vertical: 4),
            decoration: BoxDecoration(
              color: kSecAmber.withValues(alpha: 0.2),
              borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(999)),
            child: Text('LoSec mode — fine for everyday use',
              style: textTheme.labelSmall.copyWith(color: kSecAmber)),
          ),

        Spacer(),

        // Secondary controls row
        Row(mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center, children: [
          _CallControl(
            icon: call.micMuted ? Icons.mic_off_rounded : Icons.mic_rounded,
            label: call.micMuted ? 'Unmute' : 'Mute',
            onTap: call.toggleMic,
            active: !call.micMuted,
          ),
          SizedBox(width: 24),
          if (isVideoCall)
            _CallControl(
              icon: call.cameraOff ? Icons.videocam_off_rounded : Icons.videocam_rounded,
              label: call.cameraOff ? 'Camera off' : 'Camera',
              onTap: call.toggleCamera,
              active: !call.cameraOff,
            ),
          if (!isVideoCall)
            _CallControl(
              icon: call.speakerOn ? Icons.volume_up_rounded : Icons.volume_down_rounded,
              label: call.speakerOn ? 'Speaker' : 'Earpiece',
              onTap: call.toggleSpeaker,
              active: call.speakerOn,
            ),
          SizedBox(width: 24),
          _CallControl(
            icon: Icons.chat_bubble_outline_rounded,
            label: 'Chat',
            onTap: () => Navigator.pop(context),  // returns to thread
          ),
        ]),

        SizedBox(height: 32),

        // End call button (always centred, always red)
        GestureDetector(
          onTap: call.hangUp,
          child: Container(
            width: 72, height: 72,
            decoration: BoxDecoration(
              color: kSecRed,
              shape: BoxShape.circle,
              boxShadow: [BoxShadow(
                color: kSecRed.withValues(alpha: 0.4),
                blurRadius: 16, spreadRadius: 2)],
            ),
            child: Icon(Icons.call_end_rounded, size: 32, color: Colors.white),
          ),
        ),

        SizedBox(height: 48),
      ]),
    ]),
  ),
)
```

**_CallControl widget:**
```dart
GestureDetector(
  onTap: onTap,
  child: Column(mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.min, children: [
    Container(
      width: 56, height: 56,
      decoration: BoxDecoration(
        color: active == true
          ? Colors.white.withValues(alpha: 0.15)
          : Colors.white.withValues(alpha: 0.08),
        shape: BoxShape.circle,
      ),
      child: Icon(icon, size: 24,
        color: active == false
          ? Colors.white.withValues(alpha: 0.4)
          : Colors.white),
    ),
    SizedBox(height: 6),
    Text(label, style: TextStyle(fontSize: 11, color: Colors.white.withValues(alpha: 0.7))),
  ]),
)
```

**Incoming call:** Presented as a full-screen `showGeneralDialog` (not a notification UI) when the app is open. When backgrounded, uses the platform notification call UI (Android CallStyle, iOS CallKit).

```dart
// Incoming call overlay
Container(
  color: Color(0xFF0F1117),
  child: SafeArea(
    child: Column(children: [
      Spacer(),
      _ContactAvatar(peer: caller, size: 80),
      SizedBox(height: 12),
      Text(caller.resolvedDisplayName,
        style: textTheme.titleLarge.copyWith(color: Colors.white)),
      SizedBox(height: 6),
      Text('Incoming call',
        style: textTheme.bodyMedium.copyWith(
          color: Colors.white.withValues(alpha: 0.7))),
      Spacer(),
      Row(mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.spaceEvenly, children: [
        // Decline
        GestureDetector(
          onTap: call.decline,
          child: Container(
            width: 72, height: 72,
            decoration: BoxDecoration(color: kSecRed, shape: BoxShape.circle),
            child: Icon(Icons.call_end_rounded, size: 32, color: Colors.white)),
        ),
        // Accept
        GestureDetector(
          onTap: () => call.accept(CallMode.audio),
          child: Container(
            width: 72, height: 72,
            decoration: BoxDecoration(color: kSecGreen, shape: BoxShape.circle),
            child: Icon(Icons.call_rounded, size: 32, color: Colors.white)),
        ),
      ]),
      SizedBox(height: 64),
    ]),
  ),
)
```

---

#### 22.10.13 GardenProfileEditScreen

**File**: `features/communities/screens/garden_profile_edit_screen.dart`

Reached from within a Garden's member settings. Allows editing the per-Garden profile (§9.4.3) for the active membership.

**AppBar**: `'Your profile in ${garden.name}'`.

```dart
ListView(
  padding: EdgeInsets.all(16),
  children: [

    // Preview
    Card(child: Padding(
      padding: EdgeInsets.all(16),
      child: Row(children: [
        _ContactAvatar(peer: identity.thisNode.withGardenProfile(
          name: _nameController.text,
          avatar: _selectedAvatar), size: 48),
        SizedBox(width: 12),
        Expanded(child: Column(
          crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start,
          children: [
            Text(
              _nameController.text.trim().isEmpty
                ? identity.pairedProfile.displayName
                : _nameController.text,
              style: textTheme.titleSmall),
            if (_bioController.text.isNotEmpty)
              Text(_bioController.text,
                style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(
                  color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant),
                maxLines: 2, overflow: TextOverflow.ellipsis),
          ],
        )),
      ]),
    )),

    SizedBox(height: 16),

    // Display name
    TextField(
      controller: _nameController,
      decoration: InputDecoration(
        labelText: 'Display name',
        helperText: 'Leave blank to use your paired profile name.',
      ),
      maxLength: 64,
      onChanged: (_) => setState(() {}),
    ),

    SizedBox(height: 12),

    // Bio
    TextField(
      controller: _bioController,
      maxLines: 3,
      maxLength: 512,
      decoration: InputDecoration(
        labelText: 'Garden bio (optional)',
        helperText: 'Shown to members of this Garden only.',
        alignLabelWithHint: true,
      ),
    ),

    SizedBox(height: 12),

    // Pronouns
    TextField(
      controller: _pronounsController,
      decoration: InputDecoration(
        labelText: 'Pronouns (optional)',
        hintText: 'e.g. she/her, they/them',
      ),
      maxLength: 32,
    ),

    SizedBox(height: 24),

    FilledButton(
      onPressed: _save,
      style: FilledButton.styleFrom(minimumSize: Size(double.infinity, 52)),
      child: Text('Save profile'),
    ),

    SizedBox(height: 8),

    // Reset to paired profile
    TextButton(
      onPressed: _resetToDefault,
      child: Text('Use my paired profile instead'),
    ),

    SizedBox(height: 24),
  ],
)
```

`_save()` calls `bridge.setGardenProfile(gardenId, profile)`. On success, pops with a `SnackBar`: *"Your Garden profile has been updated."*

---

## Part B — UX


### 22.21 Foundational Principle

**Mesh Infinity is a chat app first.**

The default experience must look and feel like Signal or WhatsApp. You open it, you message people, it works. Security happens invisibly. Most users should never need to think about threat contexts, ratchets, or routing planes.

This is not a compromise -- it is a security requirement. **Ubiquity IS the security model.** If only activists and journalists use Mesh Infinity, every Mesh Infinity user is suspicious by association. If millions of people use it for gaming communities, family chats, and Minecraft servers, the people who genuinely need protection are invisible in the crowd. Growing the user base directly improves security for the people who need it most.

Every operational security UX decision must be evaluated against: "Can my non-technical friend who just wants to message people understand this?"

### 22.22 Plain Language Requirement

All security-related user-facing text must pass the plain language test. Technical accuracy is required; technical jargon is not.

| Technical | Plain language |
|-----------|---------------|
| "LoSec mode reduces anonymisation set via shortened inner tunnel path" | "Faster connection, slightly less private. Fine for everyday use." |
| "APNs silent push exposes notification timing metadata to Apple infrastructure" | "Apple will know when you get messages, but not what they say or who they're from." |
| "This connection uses an unverified public address without trusted-channel establishment" | "You haven't verified this person's identity yet." |
| "Self-Disavowed state broadcast to network" | "Your emergency erase has been activated. Your contacts have been notified." |
| "Cover traffic contributing to mesh anonymity set" | "Your app is sending privacy traffic to protect everyone on the network." |
| "Forward Perfect Secrecy via Double Ratchet protocol" | "Each message has its own encryption key. Old keys are automatically deleted." |
| "Inner tunnel via nested WireGuard through persistent outer tunnels" | "Your messages travel through multiple layers of encryption." |

The spec does not prescribe UI layouts or visual design -- those are frontend concerns. The spec requires that certain information be communicated in language meeting this standard.

### 22.23 Ambient State Disclosures and Onboarding

**Ambient state** disclosures describe things that are always true about the system. They are disclosed during onboarding, available in settings and help at any time, and not repeated on every action.

The following must be clearly communicated before a user first sends a message:

1. **Cover traffic:** "Your app sends extra data to keep your activity private. This uses some battery and data -- about X MB per day on average."
2. **Relay participation:** "Your device may help route other people's encrypted messages. You can't read them, and neither can anyone else."
3. **Store-and-forward:** "When your contacts are offline, their messages wait on trusted nodes until they reconnect."
4. **No central server:** "Mesh Infinity has no central servers. Your messages travel through the network itself."
5. **Trust model basics:** "You control who can contact you and what they can see. Strangers start with minimal access."

These are not scary warnings -- they are honest explanations of how the system works. Tone should be matter-of-fact and positive.

### 22.24 Event-Based Disclosures

**Event-based** disclosures describe something that just happened and the user needs to know now. They are surfaced via notification, indicator, or modal at the time of the event, with appropriate urgency based on severity, and actionable where possible.

#### 22.24.1 Security State Changes

- **Threat context change** (user-initiated): confirm the change.
- **Connection mode change to LoSec:** persistent amber indicator for the duration of the session.
- **Connection mode change to Direct:** persistent red indicator + full-screen warning (§6.7), persists during session.
- **Trusted peer becomes Disavowed:** notification: "A contact may be compromised."
- **Trusted peer becomes Compromised:** urgent notification: "A contact's device is compromised."
- **Key change approval pending:** actionable notification -- approve or decline.
- **Trust promotion request received:** actionable notification.
- **New device added to a known identity:** notification: "[Name] added a new device."

#### 22.24.2 Privacy-Reducing Actions (Require Acknowledgment Before Proceeding)

- Enabling APNs/FCM notifications (any tier above Tier 1).
- Enabling read receipts or typing indicators.
- Enabling LoSec for a service (first time per service).
- Activating direct mode (full-screen warning, §6.7).
- Cross-profile linkage (warning: irreversible).
- Sharing private profile (warning: permanent).
- Joining a Public network via QR.
- Using link share pairing (contextual warning per §8.2 threat profiles).

#### 22.24.3 Irreversible Actions (Require Explicit Confirmation)

- Public profile erasure.
- Compromised declaration.
- In-band key change approval (additional friction per §4.6).
- Killswitch activation.

#### 22.24.4 System Events (Informational, No Action Required)

- Self-Disavowed broadcast sent.
- Store-and-forward nodes holding messages for offline contacts.
- Transport migration (ambient indicator, not modal).

### 22.25 Metrics Screen

A dedicated metrics screen makes abstract security properties tangible. This is not a debugging tool -- it is a transparency tool that shows users the system working on their behalf. **Plain language labels throughout.**

**Privacy metrics:**

| Metric | Display label | Source |
|--------|-------------|--------|
| Cover traffic sent today / this week | "Privacy traffic" | Tunnel-level cover traffic counters |
| Cover packets sent vs real packets ratio | "Privacy ratio" | Packet counters |
| Peers routed for today | "Helping route messages" | Relay activity counter |
| Active outer tunnel connections | "Secure connections" | Tunnel manager state |

**Network metrics:**

| Metric | Display label | Source |
|--------|-------------|--------|
| Connected peers (direct) | "Connected friends" | Transport manager |
| Reachable peers (via mesh) | "Reachable contacts" | Routing table |
| Active transports in use | "Active transports" | Transport manager |
| Store-and-forward messages pending delivery | "Messages waiting for delivery" | S&F queue |

**Security state:**

| Metric | Display label | Source |
|--------|-------------|--------|
| Current threat context level | "Security level" | Global threat context |
| Active LoSec or Direct sessions | "Active fast connections" | Session manager |
| Pending approvals | "Pending approvals" | Key change / trust promotion queues |
| Recent security events (last 7 days) | "Recent security events" | Event log |

### 22.26 What the System Must Never Do Silently

These are hard prohibitions -- not defaults that can be changed, but behaviours that must never occur regardless of configuration:

1. Auto-downgrade security level for any connection.
2. Auto-accept any agreement, permission, or terms.
3. Auto-share location or presence information.
4. Auto-join any network or group.
5. Auto-enable any notification tier above Tier 1.
6. Auto-link profiles across contexts.
7. Auto-approve any key change.
8. Send any identifying information to any third party without explicit user awareness.
9. Enable any biometric authentication for security-critical paths (§3.10).

### 22.27 Onboarding Security Requirements

New users must complete a minimal security onboarding before first use:

1. **Ambient state disclosures** (§22.23) -- presented simply, not as a wall of text.
2. **PIN setup prompt** -- strongly encouraged, skippable with acknowledgment.
3. **Threat context selection** -- Normal / Elevated / Critical with plain-language explanations.
4. **Contact import or first pairing** -- gets the user connected immediately.

Onboarding must not:

- Require technical knowledge to complete.
- Present security choices as gatekeepers to using the app.
- Use fear-based language.
- Be skippable entirely (ambient disclosures are mandatory; the rest can be deferred).

---

### 22.28 Feature Tier Model

#### 22.28.1 Overview

Mesh Infinity exposes capability in **progressive tiers**. Tiers control what the user sees and what modules are available to enable — they are not the same as module runtime state (§17.13). A module can be enabled with its UI tier visible; a tier can be unlocked while its modules remain individually off. The UI always reflects the current module state, showing "Enable" prompts where modules are off.

The node participates fully in core mesh functions, including aiding services for others even if they are disabled locally. Routing is always active regardless of tier or module state — a node at Tier 1 still routes traffic for all other tiers transparently. See §17.13 for the full module system.

Tiers are **progressive and cumulative**: each tier unlocks everything in the tiers below it. You cannot be on Tier 3 without Tier 2 also being active. This is intentional — it reflects the natural dependency of features and prevents users from encountering half-working configurations.

Enabling a tier takes effect immediately (no restart). Sections appear in the navigation rail/bar with a 200ms fade.

**Tier unlock welcome toast:** When a user enables a new tier for the first time, a `SnackBar` appears:
```
SnackBar(
  content: Row(children: [
    Icon(_tierIcon(tier), color: Colors.white, size: 20),
    SizedBox(width: 12),
    Column(crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start, children: [
      Text('${tier.name} unlocked', style: TextStyle(fontWeight: FontWeight.w600)),
      Text(_tierOneLiner(tier), style: TextStyle(fontSize: 12)),
    ]),
  ]),
  action: SnackBarAction(label: 'Explore', onPressed: () => _navigateToTierScreen(tier)),
  duration: Duration(seconds: 5),
)
```

One-liners:
- Tier 2: "Network controls are now in your sidebar."
- Tier 3: "You can now create a private network with your devices."
- Tier 4: "Remote desktop, shell, and file services are available."
- Tier 5: "Agentic distribution and plugins are available."

The "Explore" action navigates to the Tier Discovery Screen (§22.53) filtered to the newly unlocked tier. The toast is shown once per tier, tracked in local preferences.

---

#### 22.28.2 Tier Definitions

**Tier 1 — Social** (default; always enabled; cannot be disabled)

The core product. Chat, communities, contacts. Every Mesh Infinity installation is at least Tier 1.

```
UI sections:    Chat, Communities, Files (basic — send/receive only), Contacts, Settings
Modules on:     chat (invariant), gardens (social.gardens), file_sharing (social.file_sharing),
                notifications (social.notifications)
Bandwidth use:  cover traffic, mesh routing participation, S&F for others (if social.store_forward on)
```

*Who this is:* Anyone who just wants a secure messaging app. Family group chats, friend circles, gaming communities. They should never see a router, a subnet, or a port number.

---

**Tier 2 — Network** (opt-in; requires Tier 1)

Route your own traffic through the mesh. Use your devices as exit nodes for each other. Connect to your home network remotely.

```
UI sections:    + Network section (transports, exit node selector, VPN mode toggle)
Modules available to enable:
                network.vpn_mode     — route device traffic through the mesh
                network.exit_nodes   — use trusted contacts' devices as internet exits
                network.app_connector — route specific apps only, not all traffic
                network.funnel       — expose local services to the mesh
Bandwidth use:  + VPN tunnel overhead when active
```

*Who this is:* The person who wants to use their home PC as an exit node while travelling. The person who wants secure remote access to their NAS. Someone who wants per-app routing.

---

**Tier 3 — Infinet** (opt-in; requires Tier 2)

A private virtual network for your devices and trusted people. DNS, ACLs, shared services. The mesh as your own private internet.

```
UI sections:    + Infinet section within Settings (InfinetScreen, §22.10.8)
Modules available to enable:
                network.infinet      — Infinet membership and control plane
Bandwidth use:  + Infinet gossip, state sync
```

*Who this is:* The home lab user who wants all their machines on a private network. The small team that wants a shared virtual LAN. The person who runs Tailscale today and wants something they control fully.

---

**Tier 4 — Services** (opt-in; requires Tier 3)

Host and access services across the mesh. Remote desktop, shell access, file shares, API proxies, clipboard sync.

```
UI sections:    + Services section (lists enabled protocol servers; quick-enable toggles)
                + Files section expands (full MNFP, shares management)
Modules available to enable (all from protocols group, §17.13):
                mnrdp_server, mnsp_server, mnfp_server, api_gateway,
                screen_share, clipboard_sync, print_service
                (each with its adapters as sub-toggles)
Bandwidth use:  + per active session (MNRDP: high; MNSP/MNFP: moderate; clipboard: low)
```

*Who this is:* The developer who wants shell access to their home server from anywhere. The person who wants their printer shared on the mesh. The user who wants seamless clipboard between devices.

---

**Tier 5 — Power** (opt-in; requires Tier 4)

Compartmentalised computing. Purpose devices. Qubes Air integration. Plugin runtime for third-party extensions.

```
UI sections:    + Agentic section in Infinet (purpose device management, MISLP session launcher)
                + Plugins section in Settings
Modules available to enable:
                agentic.mislp        — MISLP session handoff, purpose declarations
                plugins.runtime_enabled — plugin sandbox
Bandwidth use:  + purpose gossip when mislp enabled; per-plugin overhead
```

*Who this is:* Security-conscious users who want compartmentalisation. Qubes OS users. Developers building on the platform. People who want email on one device, browsing on another, crypto ops on a third.

---

#### 22.28.3 Bandwidth Profiles

Within any tier, the user can configure a **bandwidth profile** that adjusts how aggressively the node participates in mesh infrastructure on others' behalf. This is independent of which modules are running — it governs the invariant core's resource contribution.

```
Minimal — for metered connections, low-powered devices, or constrained deployments
  Cover traffic:   Off
  Relay for others: Off (node still routes, but does not act as a preferred relay)
  Store-and-forward: Off (does not cache messages for offline peers)
  Map gossip rate: Reduced (1/4 normal rate)

Standard — default for most users (phone on WiFi, desktop)
  Cover traffic:   On (default rate, §5.x)
  Relay for others: On
  Store-and-forward: On if social.store_forward enabled
  Map gossip rate: Normal

Generous — for always-on nodes with good connectivity (server, desktop on ethernet)
  Cover traffic:   On (elevated rate)
  Relay for others: On (preferred relay; higher routing weight)
  Store-and-forward: On with extended queue depth
  Map gossip rate: Elevated
```

Bandwidth profile is set in Settings → Network → Mesh participation. On mobile, the profile automatically drops to Minimal when on a cellular connection unless the user has explicitly set it otherwise (persisted preference: "Keep Standard on cellular").

---

#### 22.28.4 Tier State in Code

```dart
enum MeshTier { social, network, infinet, services, power }

class TierState extends ChangeNotifier {
  MeshTier _activeTier = MeshTier.social;
  BandwidthProfile _bandwidth = BandwidthProfile.standard;

  MeshTier get activeTier => _activeTier;
  BandwidthProfile get bandwidth => _bandwidth;

  bool tierUnlocked(MeshTier t) => t.index <= _activeTier.index;

  Future<void> enableTier(MeshTier t) async {
    // Must enable all prerequisite tiers
    for (final prereq in MeshTier.values.where((v) => v.index < t.index)) {
      if (!tierUnlocked(prereq)) await enableTier(prereq);
    }
    await bridge.setActiveTier(t.index);
    _activeTier = t;
    _persist();
    notifyListeners();
  }

  // Disabling a tier also disables all higher tiers (cascading)
  Future<void> disableTier(MeshTier t) async {
    for (final higher in MeshTier.values.where((v) => v.index >= t.index)) {
      await bridge.disableTierModules(higher.index);
    }
    _activeTier = MeshTier.values[t.index - 1];
    _persist();
    notifyListeners();
  }

  void _persist() => bridge.setTierConfig(
    tier: _activeTier.index,
    bandwidth: _bandwidth.index,
  );
}
```

**AppSection visibility driven by tier:**

```dart
List<AppSection> get _visibleSections {
  return [
    AppSection.chat,
    AppSection.communities,
    AppSection.files,
    AppSection.contacts,
    if (tier.tierUnlocked(MeshTier.network)) AppSection.network,
    AppSection.settings,
  ];
}
```

**Settings sections gated by tier:**

| Settings section | Minimum tier |
|-----------------|-------------|
| Notifications | Tier 1 |
| Identity / Masks | Tier 1 |
| Multi-device | Tier 1 |
| Mesh participation (bandwidth profile) | Tier 1 |
| VPN / exit nodes | Tier 2 |
| App Connector | Tier 2 |
| Infinet | Tier 3 |
| Services (protocol servers) | Tier 4 |
| Agentic / MISLP | Tier 5 |
| Plugins | Tier 5 |

Sections not yet unlocked are hidden entirely, not greyed out. The user discovers them by activating the next tier — or via the "What else can Mesh Infinity do?" explainer accessible from the Settings footer.

---

### 22.29 Multi-Device Sync Disclosures

When a user adds a second device, the following must be clearly communicated before or during the pairing flow:

1. **Key transfer:** "When you add a device, your identity keys are copied to it over an encrypted channel. Both devices will have equal access to your identity."
2. **History not automatic:** "Your message history stays on each device. You can share history from an existing device manually — it's never automatic."
3. **Key rotation on removal:** "Removing a device rotates your keys. Your contacts won't notice any change, but all active sessions will refresh."
4. **Primary device:** "Your first device is the primary. Only the primary device can authorize new device additions."
5. **Independent ratchets:** "Each device maintains its own message encryption state. A message read on one device doesn't automatically decrypt it on another — it just marks it as read."

These disclosures are presented as a brief inline explainer within the MultiDeviceScreen pairing flow (§22.10.7), not as a blocking modal. Plain language required (§22.22).

### 22.30 VPN Mode and App Connector Disclosures

When a user enables Mesh VPN mode (§22.9.4), the following must be communicated:

1. **Traffic routing:** "Apps you select will have their traffic routed through the mesh network. This encrypts the traffic within the mesh, but the exit node (if configured) can see your destinations unless you use Tor routing."
2. **Android VPN key:** "Android will show a VPN key icon in the notification bar. This is expected and does not indicate a security problem."
3. **Not a commercial VPN:** "Mesh VPN is not a commercial VPN service. It routes traffic through people you trust — not a company's servers. Your anonymity depends on who your exit node is."
4. **Battery and data:** "VPN mode keeps a persistent background connection and uses additional battery and data."
5. **iOS limitations:** (iOS only) "iOS restricts background VPN connections. Mesh VPN mode on iOS may disconnect when the app is backgrounded for extended periods."

These disclosures appear in the consent dialog shown before first activation (§22.9.4) and are available at any time via an info button in the App Connector screen.

### 22.31 Trust Level UX

Trust levels are a powerful concept that most users should never need to think about consciously. The UX goal is to make trust feel like a natural extension of relationship management, not a technical configuration step.

**The key distinction the UI must communicate:**

> Pairing = you know how to reach them. Trusting = you're sharing your identity with them.

This distinction maps to the protocol's Acquaintance (Level 5) vs Trusted (Level 6) boundary. The UI must never refer to "Level 5" or "Level 6" in user-facing text — only to the relationship concept these levels represent.

**Plain language mappings for trust levels:**

| Internal level | User-facing label | Short description |
|---------------|------------------|-------------------|
| 0 Unknown | — (not shown as a named level) | "Not yet in your contacts" |
| 1 Public | "New contact" | "Just paired — you've exchanged keys" |
| 2 Vouched | "Introduced" | "Vouched for by someone you trust" |
| 3 Referenced | "Recommended" | "Highly recommended by a trusted contact" |
| 4 Ally | "Trusted introduction" | "Introduced by your closest contacts" |
| 5 Acquaintance | "Contact" | "You've paired and can chat" |
| 6 Trusted | "Trusted" | "Sharing your identity — private profile exchanged" |
| 7 HighlyTrusted | "Close trusted" | "Elevated trust — routing and storage access" |
| 8 InnerCircle | "Inner Circle" | "Maximum trust" |

**Trust promotion UX flow:**

When a user elevates a contact to Level 6 (Trusted), a brief explanation card appears before the confirmation:

```
Card — shown inline in the trust selector, replaces the description line:
  Icon: lock_outlined (kBrand)
  Title: "Share your identity with [name]?"
  Body: "Trusting [name] shares your private profile — your real name and
         any contact info you've added — with them. They can also share
         encrypted messages with your trusted channel.
         This is reversible, but they keep any profile info already shared."
  Actions: [Cancel] [Trust [name]]
```

The confirmation uses `[name]` (resolved display name), not "this contact." The wording is warm and concrete, not legalistic.

**When the other party hasn't reciprocated:**

After promoting to Level 6, if the remote peer has not yet responded, the ContactDetailScreen trust section shows:

```
Container(
  padding: EdgeInsets.all(12),
  decoration: BoxDecoration(
    color: kSecAmber.withValues(alpha: 0.08),
    borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(8)),
  child: Row(children: [
    Icon(Icons.hourglass_empty_outlined, size: 16, color: kSecAmber),
    SizedBox(width: 8),
    Expanded(child: Text(
      'Waiting for [name] to trust you back. '
      'Your private profile will be shared once they do.',
      style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(color: kSecAmber))),
  ]),
)
```

**Trust downgrade UX:**

Reducing trust (e.g. from Level 6 back to Level 5) shows a one-sentence confirmation: *"Removing trust from [name] will disconnect the private channel. They'll no longer receive your private profile updates."* Two buttons: Cancel / Remove trust. No dramatic warning beyond this — the plain explanation is sufficient.

### 22.32 Message Request UX

Message requests (§22.5.4) must feel reassuring, not alarming. The default framing is "someone new wants to talk to you," not "potential threat detected."

**Principles:**

1. **Not scary.** Message requests are normal. Most are from people who legitimately paired with the user and initiated a conversation. The UI should not use words like "unknown," "unverified," or "untrusted" in this context.

2. **Context provided.** The request tile shows when and how the peer was paired, what trust level they're at, and their paired profile (if set). This gives the user enough context to make a decision without needing to look anything up.

3. **Declining is frictionless.** Declining a request is a single tap. No confirmation dialog. No explanation required. A brief `SnackBar` with Undo is all the feedback needed.

4. **Badge, not interruption.** The message requests badge in the AppBar is a passive indicator, not a red dot designed to create urgency. It should look like an informational state, not an alert. Use `outline` colour, not `error`, for the badge when request count > 0. Only use `error` colour if the count has been sitting above zero for more than 24 hours without the user checking.

5. **Auto-dismiss on reply.** If the user opens a request, reads the message, and sends a reply, the request is automatically accepted. Accepting and replying are the same action.

### 22.33 Haptic Feedback Requirements

Haptic feedback makes interactions feel physical and confirms actions that happen without a visible state change. All haptics use `HapticFeedback` from `flutter/services.dart`.

| Trigger | Haptic type | Notes |
|---------|------------|-------|
| Message sent | `lightImpact` | Confirms transmission |
| Message received (foreground) | None | Audio/visual notification is sufficient |
| Swipe-to-reply (threshold reached) | `selectionClick` | Confirms swipe threshold |
| Swipe-to-archive (threshold reached) | `mediumImpact` | Slightly stronger — destructive-ish action |
| Long-press message (action sheet opens) | `mediumImpact` | Confirms long-press |
| Reaction added | `lightImpact` | Quick |
| Request accepted | `notificationSuccess` | Positive confirmation |
| Request declined | `mediumImpact` | Neutral |
| Wrong PIN entered | `notificationError` | Negative feedback |
| PIN correct | `notificationSuccess` | Entry unlocked |
| Trust level changed | `selectionClick` | Subtle selector feedback |
| Emergency erase confirmed | None | This action is too serious for haptic celebration |
| Call started | `notificationSuccess` | |
| Call ended | `lightImpact` | |
| Copy to clipboard | `selectionClick` | Standard system pattern |

**Platform notes:**
- iOS: all haptic types map naturally to `UIImpactFeedbackGenerator` / `UINotificationFeedbackGenerator`
- Android: `vibrate()` (50ms) for light/selection, `vibrate()` (100ms) for medium, platform notification haptic for success/error
- Desktop (macOS/Windows/Linux): No haptics — skip all calls silently

### 22.34 Accessibility Requirements

Mesh Infinity must meet WCAG 2.1 AA. The following requirements are non-negotiable:

**Colour contrast:**
- Body text on any background: minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio
- Large text and UI elements: minimum 3:1
- Status indicators (trust badges, security status bar) must not rely on colour alone — always pair colour with an icon or label

**Touch targets:**
- Minimum 48×48 logical pixels for any interactive element
- Exception: secondary icons within a ListTile trailing area may be 40×40 if `constraints: BoxConstraints(minWidth: 40, minHeight: 40)` is set

**Screen reader support:**
- All `IconButton`s must have a `tooltip` (doubles as semantic label)
- Custom widgets that are interactive must set `Semantics(label: ..., button: true)`
- `MaskAvatar` must expose `Semantics(label: '${mask.name} avatar')`
- `TrustBadge` must expose `Semantics(label: '${level.label} trust level')`
- `OnlineIndicator` must expose `Semantics(label: 'Online')`
- Message bubbles must be accessible: `Semantics(label: '${senderName}: ${message.body}, ${_formatTime(message.sentAt)}')`
- Security status bar must announce its text content when it appears: use `SemanticsService.announce`

**Dynamic text scaling:**
- All text uses Material 3 `TextTheme` — font sizes scale with `textScaleFactor` automatically
- Exception: monospace fields (peer IDs, keys, codes) use fixed `fontSize` values — they must remain readable but exact size matters for layout
- Bottom navigation labels use `WidgetStateProperty` and must be visible at 1.5× scale without overflow

**Reduced motion:**
- Respect `MediaQuery.of(context).disableAnimations`
