| App launch failed on remote | "Couldn't start [app name] on [device]." | Yes |

**Empty states:**

| Screen | Icon | Title | Body |
|--------|------|-------|------|
| PurposeLauncherSheet (no purposes declared) | `devices_outlined` | "No devices set up" | "Assign a purpose to a device in your Infinet settings." |
| AppGalleryScreen (no apps) | `apps_outlined` | "No apps available" | "This device hasn't registered any apps for streaming." |
| SessionTaskbar (no sessions) | — | (not shown — taskbar is hidden) | — |

---

### 22.53 Tier Discovery Screen

**File:** `features/settings/screens/tier_discovery_screen.dart`

The "What else can Mesh Infinity do?" screen. Accessible from the Settings footer ("Explore features"), from the `ModuleEnablePrompt` (§22.52.7) "Learn more" link, and from a first-run nudge after 7 days at Tier 1. Shows what each tier adds, in plain language, with a one-tap unlock button.

**AppBar:** `'Explore features'`, leading `CloseButton` (if pushed modally) or `BackButton` (if pushed normally).

```dart
ListView(padding: EdgeInsets.all(16), children: [

  Padding(
    padding: EdgeInsets.only(bottom: 16),
    child: Text(
      'Mesh Infinity grows with you. Start with chat and add features '
      'when they make sense for how you use it.',
      style: textTheme.bodyMedium.copyWith(
        color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant))),

  for (final tier in MeshTier.values)
    _TierCard(
      tier: tier,
      isActive: tierState.tierUnlocked(tier),
      onEnable: tier.index > tierState.activeTier.index
        ? () => _enableTier(context, tier)
        : null),
])
```

**`_TierCard`:**

```dart
Card(
  margin: EdgeInsets.only(bottom: 12),
  child: Column(crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start, children: [

    // Header strip in tier colour
    Container(
      width: double.infinity,
      padding: EdgeInsets.fromLTRB(16, 12, 16, 12),
      decoration: BoxDecoration(
        color: _tierColour(tier).withValues(alpha: 0.12),
        borderRadius: BorderRadius.vertical(top: Radius.circular(16))),
      child: Row(children: [
        Icon(_tierIcon(tier), size: 20, color: _tierColour(tier)),
        SizedBox(width: 10),
        Expanded(child: Text(_tierName(tier),
          style: textTheme.titleSmall.copyWith(color: _tierColour(tier)))),
        if (isActive)
          Container(
            padding: EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 8, vertical: 3),
            decoration: BoxDecoration(
              color: kSecGreen.withValues(alpha: 0.15),
              borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(999)),
            child: Text('Active',
              style: textTheme.labelSmall.copyWith(color: kSecGreen))),
      ]),
    ),

    Padding(
      padding: EdgeInsets.all(16),
      child: Column(crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start, children: [
        Text(_tierTagline(tier),
          style: textTheme.bodyMedium),
        SizedBox(height: 10),
        // Feature bullets (3-4 items max per tier — not exhaustive)
        for (final feature in _tierFeatures(tier))
          Padding(
            padding: EdgeInsets.only(bottom: 4),
            child: Row(crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start, children: [
              Icon(Icons.check_rounded, size: 14, color: _tierColour(tier)),
              SizedBox(width: 8),
              Expanded(child: Text(feature,
                style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(
                  color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant))),
            ])),
        if (onEnable != null) ...[
          SizedBox(height: 16),
          FilledButton(
            onPressed: onEnable,
            style: FilledButton.styleFrom(
              backgroundColor: _tierColour(tier),
              minimumSize: Size(double.infinity, 44)),
            child: Text('Unlock ${_tierName(tier)}')),
        ],
      ]),
    ),
  ]),
)
```

**Tier metadata:**

```dart
String _tierName(MeshTier t) => const [
  'Social', 'Network', 'Infinet', 'Services', 'Power'
][t.index];

IconData _tierIcon(MeshTier t) => const [
  Icons.chat_outlined,
  Icons.vpn_key_outlined,
  Icons.lan_outlined,
  Icons.settings_ethernet_outlined,
  Icons.security_outlined,
][t.index];

Color _tierColour(MeshTier t) => [
  kBrand,
  kSecGreen,
  kSecAmber,
  Color(0xFF8B5CF6),   // purple
  kSecRed,
][t.index];

String _tierTagline(MeshTier t) => const [
  'Chat, communities, and contacts. Everything a secure messaging app should be.',
  'Route your traffic through the mesh. Use your devices as your own VPN.',
  'A private virtual network for your devices. Your own internet, on your terms.',
  'Stream apps, share files, and expose services across your mesh.',
  'Compartmentalise your digital life across purpose-built devices.',
][t.index];

List<String> _tierFeatures(MeshTier t) => const [
  ['Encrypted messaging', 'Communities (Gardens)', 'File sharing', 'Secure contacts'],
  ['Mesh VPN — route your traffic through friends\' devices', 'Per-app routing', 'Exit node support', 'Local service exposure (Funnel)'],
  ['Private virtual LAN for all your devices', 'Mesh DNS — your devices get their own domain names', 'Access control lists', 'Shared services across devices'],
  ['Remote desktop — access any device\'s screen', 'Remote shell — terminal access anywhere', 'Shared file access (MNFP)', 'API and print service sharing'],
  ['Purpose devices — email on one machine, browsing on another', 'Qubes Air integration — compartmentalised sessions', 'Split operations (SSH keys, crypto keys never leave their device)', 'Plugin runtime for third-party extensions'],
][t.index];
```

**Unlock flow:** Tapping "Unlock [tier]" shows a brief confirmation sheet (not a full dialog — bottom sheet):

```dart
showModalBottomSheet(child: Padding(
  padding: EdgeInsets.fromLTRB(16, 8, 16, 32),
  child: Column(mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.min, children: [
    Text('Unlock ${_tierName(tier)}?', style: textTheme.titleMedium),
    SizedBox(height: 8),
    Text(
      'This enables the ${_tierName(tier)} tier. '
      'Individual features within it are still off by default — '
      'you choose what to turn on.',
      style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)),
    SizedBox(height: 16),
    Row(children: [
      Expanded(child: OutlinedButton(
        onPressed: () => Navigator.pop(context),
        child: Text('Not now'))),
      SizedBox(width: 12),
      Expanded(child: FilledButton(
        onPressed: () {
          Navigator.pop(context);
          tierState.enableTier(tier);
          // Show a SnackBar with what's now available
          ScaffoldMessenger.of(context).showSnackBar(SnackBar(
            content: Text('${_tierName(tier)} unlocked. '
              'Find new options in Settings.')));
        },
        style: FilledButton.styleFrom(backgroundColor: _tierColour(tier)),
        child: Text('Unlock'))),
    ]),
  ]),
))
```

**Settings footer entry:**

In `SettingsScreen`, below all settings groups, a persistent footer link:

```dart
Padding(
  padding: EdgeInsets.fromLTRB(16, 8, 16, 24),
  child: OutlinedButton.icon(
    onPressed: () => Navigator.push(context,
      MaterialPageRoute(builder: (_) => TierDiscoveryScreen())),
    icon: Icon(Icons.explore_outlined, size: 18),
    label: Text('Explore features'),
    style: OutlinedButton.styleFrom(
      minimumSize: Size(double.infinity, 44))),
)
```

### 22.54 Services Screen (Tier 4)

**File:** `features/services/screens/services_screen.dart`

The Services section is a Tier 4 navigation destination (§22.28). It lists all §20 protocol servers and their current state. This is the primary UI for enabling, configuring, and monitoring hosted services.

**AppBar:** `'Services'`, no leading (it's a top-level nav destination). Action: `IconButton(Icons.help_outline)` → opens a brief explainer sheet.

**Body:**

```dart
ListView(padding: EdgeInsets.all(16), children: [

  // Module disabled banner — if protocols module group is entirely off
  if (!modules.protocols.anyEnabled)
    Padding(
      padding: EdgeInsets.only(bottom: 12),
      child: ErrorBanner(
        message: 'No services are running. Enable a service below to start hosting.',
        onRetry: null,  // no retry — just informational
      )),

  SectionHeader('Remote Access'),
  _ServiceTile(
    icon: Icons.desktop_windows_outlined,
    title: 'Remote Desktop',
    subtitle: 'Stream your screen or individual apps to trusted contacts.',
    enabled: modules.protocols.mnrdpServer,
    onToggle: (v) => modules.setMnrdpServer(v),
    onConfigure: () => Navigator.push(context,
      MaterialPageRoute(builder: (_) => MnrdpServerConfigScreen())),
  ),
  _ServiceTile(
    icon: Icons.terminal_outlined,
    title: 'Remote Shell',
    subtitle: 'Secure terminal access from any mesh device.',
    enabled: modules.protocols.mnspServer,
    onToggle: (v) => modules.setMnspServer(v),
    onConfigure: () => Navigator.push(context,
      MaterialPageRoute(builder: (_) => MnspServerConfigScreen())),
  ),

  SizedBox(height: 8),
  SectionHeader('Files & Data'),
  _ServiceTile(
    icon: Icons.folder_shared_outlined,
    title: 'File Access',
    subtitle: 'Share folders and drives with mesh contacts.',
    enabled: modules.protocols.mnfpServer,
    onToggle: (v) => modules.setMnfpServer(v),
    onConfigure: () => Navigator.push(context,
      MaterialPageRoute(builder: (_) => MnfpSharesConfigScreen())),
  ),
  _ServiceTile(
    icon: Icons.api_outlined,
    title: 'API Gateway',
    subtitle: 'Expose local HTTP, gRPC, or WebSocket services to the mesh.',
    enabled: modules.protocols.apiGateway,
    onToggle: (v) => modules.setApiGateway(v),
    onConfigure: () => Navigator.push(context,
      MaterialPageRoute(builder: (_) => ApiGatewayConfigScreen())),
  ),

  SizedBox(height: 8),
  SectionHeader('Sharing'),
  _ServiceTile(
    icon: Icons.content_copy_outlined,
    title: 'Clipboard Sync',
    subtitle: 'Keep clipboard in sync across your devices.',
    enabled: modules.protocols.clipboardSync,
    onToggle: (v) => modules.setClipboardSync(v),
    onConfigure: null,  // no configuration — auto-connects to personal Infinet devices
  ),
  _ServiceTile(
    icon: Icons.monitor_outlined,
    title: 'Screen Share',
    subtitle: 'Share a view-only display with contacts.',
    enabled: modules.protocols.screenShare,
    onToggle: (v) => modules.setScreenShare(v),
    onConfigure: null,
  ),
  _ServiceTile(
    icon: Icons.print_outlined,
    title: 'Print Services',
    subtitle: 'Share printers with mesh contacts.',
    enabled: modules.protocols.printService,
    onToggle: (v) => modules.setPrintService(v),
    onConfigure: () => Navigator.push(context,
      MaterialPageRoute(builder: (_) => PrintServiceConfigScreen())),
  ),

  SizedBox(height: 24),

  // Active sessions summary (if any services running)
  if (services.hasActiveSessions) ...[
    SectionHeader('Active sessions'),
    for (final session in services.activeSessions)
      ListTile(
        dense: true,
        leading: Icon(_sessionTypeIcon(session.type), size: 18,
          color: kBrand),
        title: Text(session.peerName,
          style: textTheme.titleSmall),
        subtitle: Text(
          '${_sessionTypeName(session.type)} · ${_formatElapsed(session.startedAt)}',
          style: textTheme.bodySmall),
        trailing: TextButton(
          onPressed: () => services.terminateSession(session.id),
          child: Text('End', style: TextStyle(color: colorScheme.error))),
      ),
  ],

  SizedBox(height: 24),
])
```

**`_ServiceTile`:**

```dart
Card(
  margin: EdgeInsets.only(bottom: 8),
  child: ListTile(
    contentPadding: EdgeInsets.fromLTRB(16, 8, 8, 8),
    leading: Container(
      width: 40, height: 40,
      decoration: BoxDecoration(
        color: enabled
          ? kBrand.withValues(alpha: 0.12)
          : colorScheme.surfaceContainerHighest,
        borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(10)),
      child: Icon(icon, size: 20,
        color: enabled ? kBrand : colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)),
    title: Text(title, style: textTheme.titleSmall),
    subtitle: Text(subtitle,
      style: textTheme.bodySmall.copyWith(
        color: colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant)),
    trailing: Row(mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.min, children: [
      if (onConfigure != null && enabled)
        IconButton(
          icon: Icon(Icons.tune_outlined, size: 18),
          onPressed: onConfigure,
          tooltip: 'Configure',
          constraints: BoxConstraints(minWidth: 36, minHeight: 36)),
      Switch(value: enabled, onChanged: onToggle),
    ]),
    isThreeLine: true,
  ),
)
```

**Nav bar integration:** The Services section appears in the nav bar when `tier.tierUnlocked(MeshTier.services)`. `AppSection.services` is added to `_visibleSections` between Files and Contacts.

### 22.55 Settings Screen Updates

Additions to `SettingsScreen` (§22.10.1) that weren't previously specced:

#### 22.55.1 Mesh Participation Section

Shown at all tiers. Controls the bandwidth profile (§22.28.3).

```dart
SectionHeader('Mesh participation'),
ListTile(
  leading: Icon(Icons.hub_outlined),
  title: Text('Bandwidth profile'),
  subtitle: Text(_bandwidthProfileLabel(bandwidth)),
  trailing: Icon(Icons.chevron_right),
  onTap: () => _openBandwidthSheet(context),
),
```

Bandwidth profile sheet:
```dart
showModalBottomSheet(child: Column(mainAxisSize: MainAxisSize.min, children: [
  Padding(padding: EdgeInsets.fromLTRB(16, 4, 16, 8),
    child: Text('Mesh participation', style: textTheme.titleMedium)),
  for (final profile in BandwidthProfile.values)
    RadioListTile<BandwidthProfile>(
      value: profile,
      groupValue: bandwidth,
      onChanged: (v) { tierState.setBandwidth(v!); Navigator.pop(context); },
      title: Text(_bandwidthProfileLabel(profile)),
      subtitle: Text(_bandwidthProfileDesc(profile))),
  SizedBox(height: 8),
]))
```

Descriptions:
- **Minimal** — "Metered or battery-constrained. Only essential mesh functions."
- **Standard** — "Balanced. Helps route traffic for others. Recommended."
- **Generous** — "Always-on device with good connectivity. Maximum mesh contribution."

#### 22.55.2 Features Section

Shows tier unlock state and links to `TierDiscoveryScreen`.

```dart
SectionHeader('Features'),
ListTile(
  leading: Icon(Icons.explore_outlined),
  title: Text('Explore features'),
  subtitle: Text('You\'re on Tier ${tierState.activeTier.index + 1}: '
    '${_tierName(tierState.activeTier)}'),
  trailing: Icon(Icons.chevron_right),
  onTap: () => Navigator.push(context,
    MaterialPageRoute(builder: (_) => TierDiscoveryScreen())),
),
```

### 22.56 Debug & Testing Menu (Debug Builds Only)

**File:** `features/settings/screens/debug_screen.dart`

**Availability:** Visible ONLY in debug builds (`kDebugMode == true`). In release builds, this screen does not exist — no code path leads to it, and it is tree-shaken by the compiler.

**Access:** Settings screen → scroll to bottom → "Developer Options" tile (only in debug builds). Guarded by `if (kDebugMode)` in the settings list builder.

**Purpose:** Provides developers and testers with tools for:
- Inspecting internal state without attaching a debugger
- Simulating network conditions, threat contexts, and failure modes
- Dumping diagnostic logs for bug reports
- Exercising protocol flows that are hard to trigger naturally

#### 22.56.1 Log Viewer

An in-app log viewer with ring buffer (last 5,000 entries, ~2 MB cap).

```dart
// Log entry model
class LogEntry {
  final DateTime timestamp;
  final LogLevel level;    // trace, debug, info, warn, error
  final String module;     // e.g. "crypto.x3dh", "routing.solver", "ffi"
  final String message;
  final Map<String, dynamic>? metadata;  // optional structured data
}

enum LogLevel { trace, debug, info, warn, error }
```

**UI:**
```
AppBar(
  title: Text('Logs'),
  actions: [
    // Filter by level
    PopupMenuButton<LogLevel>(
      icon: Icon(Icons.filter_list),
      onSelected: (level) => debugState.setMinLevel(level),
      itemBuilder: (_) => LogLevel.values.map((l) =>
        PopupMenuItem(value: l, child: Text(l.name.toUpperCase()))).toList(),
    ),
    // Filter by module
    IconButton(icon: Icon(Icons.category_outlined),
      onPressed: () => _showModuleFilter(context)),
    // Copy all visible logs
    IconButton(icon: Icon(Icons.copy_all),
      onPressed: () => _copyLogs(context)),
    // Clear logs
    IconButton(icon: Icon(Icons.delete_outline),
      onPressed: () => debugState.clearLogs()),
  ],
)

// Body: reverse ListView of log entries
ListView.builder(
  reverse: true,  // newest at bottom, auto-scroll
  itemCount: debugState.filteredLogs.length,
  itemBuilder: (_, i) {
    final log = debugState.filteredLogs[i];
    return ListTile(
      dense: true,
      leading: Icon(_levelIcon(log.level), size: 16,
        color: _levelColor(log.level)),
      title: Text('${log.module}: ${log.message}',
        style: TextStyle(fontFamily: 'monospace', fontSize: 12)),
      subtitle: Text(
        '${log.timestamp.toIso8601String()} ${log.metadata ?? ""}',
        style: TextStyle(fontSize: 10, color: Colors.grey)),
    );
  },
)
```

Log levels:
- **trace** — per-packet, per-ratchet-step. Very verbose. Off by default.
- **debug** — function entry/exit, state transitions. On in debug builds.
- **info** — significant events (peer connected, message sent, rekey completed).
- **warn** — recoverable issues (retry, fallback, degraded state).
- **error** — failures (send failed, decrypt failed, connection lost).

Backend logging: Rust backend writes logs to a ring buffer accessible via FFI (`mi_get_logs(min_level, since_timestamp) -> JSON`). The Flutter log viewer polls this on a 1-second timer when the log screen is visible.

#### 22.56.2 State Inspector

Displays raw internal state for debugging. Read-only. Each section is an expandable card.

```
_InspectorSection('Identity', [
  _KV('Mesh Identity', meshId.publicBytes.hex),
  _KV('Self Identity', selfId?.peerId.hex ?? 'locked'),
  _KV('Active Masks', '${masks.length}'),
  _KV('Identity Unlocked', '${ctx.identityUnlocked}'),
  _KV('PIN Attempts', '${pin.attempts}'),
]),
_InspectorSection('Routing', [
  _KV('Public Routes', '${routingTable.public.length}'),
  _KV('Group Routes', '${routingTable.groups.length}'),
  _KV('Local Routes', '${routingTable.local.length}'),
  _KV('BLE Ephemeral', '${routingTable.bleEphemeral.length}'),
  _KV('Dedup Cache', '${dedupCache.len()}'),
]),
_InspectorSection('Transport', [
  _KV('Active Tunnels', '${tunnelCount}'),
  _KV('Solver Mode', solverMode),
  _KV('Cover Traffic', '${coverRate} B/s'),
  _KV('KCP Sessions', '${kcpSessions}'),
]),
_InspectorSection('Sessions', [
  _KV('Active Sessions', '${sessions.count}'),
  _KV('Ratchet Steps', '${totalRatchetSteps}'),
  _KV('Skipped Keys', '${skippedKeyCount}'),
]),
_InspectorSection('Storage', [
  _KV('Vault Collections', '${vault.collectionCount}'),
  _KV('S&F Pending', '${sfPending}'),
  _KV('Relay Deposits', '${relayCount}'),
  _KV('Total Storage', '${formatBytes(totalBytes)}'),
]),
_InspectorSection('Contacts', [
  _KV('Total Peers', '${contactCount}'),
  _KV('Trusted (L6+)', '${trustedCount}'),
  _KV('Blocked', '${blockedCount}'),
]),
_InspectorSection('Trusted Contexts', [
  _KV('Tailscale', '${tailscaleTrusted ? "ON" : "OFF"}'),
  _KV('ZeroTier', '${zerotierTrusted ? "ON" : "OFF"}'),
  _KV('LAN', '${lanTrusted ? "ON" : "OFF"}'),
  _KV('mDNS on Trusted', '${mdnsOnTrusted ? "ON" : "OFF"}'),
]),
```

#### 22.56.3 Simulation Controls

Allows testers to simulate conditions that are hard to reproduce naturally.

```
SectionHeader('Simulate'),

// Threat context override
ListTile(
  leading: Icon(Icons.shield_outlined),
  title: Text('Override Threat Context'),
  subtitle: Text('Currently: ${threatContext.name}'),
  trailing: SegmentedButton<ThreatContext>(...),
),

// Network partition
SwitchListTile(
  title: Text('Simulate Network Partition'),
  subtitle: Text('Drop all outbound packets'),
  value: debugState.networkPartition,
  onChanged: (v) => debugState.setNetworkPartition(v),
),

// Latency injection
ListTile(
  title: Text('Inject Latency'),
  subtitle: Text('${debugState.injectedLatencyMs}ms added to all sends'),
  trailing: Slider(value: debugState.injectedLatencyMs.toDouble(),
    min: 0, max: 5000, divisions: 50,
    onChanged: (v) => debugState.setInjectedLatency(v.toInt())),
),

// Force rekey
ListTile(
  title: Text('Force Session Rekey'),
  subtitle: Text('Triggers immediate rekey on all active sessions'),
  trailing: ElevatedButton(onPressed: debugState.forceRekey,
    child: Text('Rekey Now')),
),

// Trigger killswitch test (creates temporary identity)
ListTile(
  title: Text('Test Killswitch (Safe)'),
  subtitle: Text('Creates a temporary identity, erases it, verifies cleanup'),
  trailing: ElevatedButton(
    onPressed: debugState.testKillswitch,
    child: Text('Run Test'),
    style: ElevatedButton.styleFrom(backgroundColor: Colors.orange)),
),

// Dump state to JSON
ListTile(
  title: Text('Export Debug Dump'),
  subtitle: Text('Full state snapshot as JSON (no key material)'),
  trailing: IconButton(icon: Icon(Icons.download),
    onPressed: () => debugState.exportDump()),
),
```

#### 22.56.4 Protocol Exerciser

Sends test messages through specific protocol paths for verification.

```
SectionHeader('Protocol Tests'),

_TestButton('X3DH Handshake', 'Run a full X3DH key agreement with self',
  onRun: debugState.testX3dh),
_TestButton('Double Ratchet', 'Send 100 messages through ratchet, verify decrypt',
  onRun: debugState.testDoubleRatchet),
_TestButton('4-Layer Encrypt', 'Encrypt and decrypt a test message through all 4 layers',
  onRun: debugState.testFourLayer),
_TestButton('Sender Keys', 'Create a group, distribute sender keys, verify group decrypt',
  onRun: debugState.testSenderKeys),
_TestButton('S&F Round-Trip', 'Deposit a message in S&F, retrieve it, verify integrity',
  onRun: debugState.testStoreForward),
_TestButton('Relay Deposit', 'Deposit and retrieve with HMAC gate verification',
  onRun: debugState.testRelayDeposit),
_TestButton('Safety Numbers', 'Compute and verify safety numbers for a test keypair',
  onRun: debugState.testSafetyNumbers),
_TestButton('Sigma Handshake', 'Run Sigma protocol with self, verify proof',
  onRun: debugState.testSigma),
```

Each test button shows a result indicator: green check (pass), red X (fail), or spinner (running). Results include timing information.

#### 22.56.5 FFI Call Tracer

Records all FFI calls crossing the Dart↔Rust boundary with timing.

```
SwitchListTile(
  title: Text('FFI Call Tracing'),
  subtitle: Text('Records all FFI calls with timing (impacts performance)'),
  value: debugState.ffiTracingEnabled,
  onChanged: (v) => debugState.setFfiTracing(v),
),
```

When enabled, every `BackendBridge` method wraps its FFI call with:
```dart
final start = DateTime.now();
final result = _actualFfiCall(...);
final elapsed = DateTime.now().difference(start);
debugState.recordFfiCall(functionName, elapsed, resultSize);
```

The trace view shows: function name, call count, avg/p50/p99 latency, total bytes returned.

#### 22.56.6 Build Info

Static build information for bug reports.

```
_InspectorSection('Build', [
  _KV('App Version', packageInfo.version),
  _KV('Build Number', packageInfo.buildNumber),
  _KV('Rust Backend', rustVersion),
  _KV('Flutter', flutterVersion),
  _KV('Dart', Platform.version),
  _KV('Platform', '${Platform.operatingSystem} ${Platform.operatingSystemVersion}'),
  _KV('Debug Mode', '${kDebugMode}'),
  _KV('Profile Mode', '${kProfileMode}'),
]),
```
